_comment: Macro-Terrain: White Tiger Fang Peaks. Per scale directive: this terrain IS a being. Player shouldn't initially realize they're standing on bone. Divine Sense -> Perception changes -> 'You realize this mountain range... is one finger.' Prime Directive: the being's corpse is objectively terrain; perception tier determines what the player sees.
name: White Tiger Fang Peaks
scale: 50 li across — the shed fangs of a divine beast.
canonical_event: The White Tiger shed its fangs here before ascending to the Foreign Battleground.

perception_tiered_appearance.mortal: A cluster of sharp white peaks, 50 li across, stone white-gold, air carries killing intent.
perception_tiered_appearance.qi_condensation: The peaks radiate slaughter-qi; cultivators feel uneasy here.
perception_tiered_appearance.foundation_establishment: The peaks are petrified fangs; the slaughter-qi is residual divine intent.
perception_tiered_appearance.core_formation: This is the White Tiger's shed fang cluster; the peaks formed from its teeth.
perception_tiered_appearance.nascent_soul: The slaughter-essence still saturates the peaks; cultivating here grants slaughter-essence.
perception_tiered_appearance.spirit_severing: The White Tiger's killing intent lingers; the divine beast returns at world-wars.

perception_mechanic.trigger: When player's perception tier rises, the appearance re-rolls automatically.
perception_mechanic.no_locked_upgrades: A mortal who later reaches Core Formation will see the truth; the terrain does not change, only understanding does.
perception_mechanic.canon_fidelity: This is exactly how Wang Lin perceived Ancient God Tu Si's remains — first as terrain, then as god-bone, then as god-memory.
